lines: Welcome to the Civic Nightmare Assistance System. I am C.L.A.U.D.I.A.
Civic Logistic Automated Universal Diplomatic Intelligence Assistant. Yes, it's a stretch.
Your case: you need to renew your passport. Simple, right?
Wrong. Due to regulation ZX-9001, you need signatures from 6 world leaders.
Start with Trump. Oval building, northeast. Fair warning: his ego barely fits in there.
You survived. I'm genuinely surprised.
Trump signed... with a golden marker. Of course he did.
Next: Elon Musk. Diamond-shaped building, northwest. Don't accept any crypto payments.
Still on planet Earth? Good. Musk didn't launch you into orbit?
His signature looks like a QR code, but I'll accept it.
Next: Von der Leyen. Palace with cut corners, east. Bring patience. A LOT of patience.
18 months of bureaucracy compressed into 5 minutes. Peak European efficiency.
Her signature came with 47 pages of regulatory appendix. Wonderful.
Now Putin. Cross-shaped building, west. Smile. Don't make sudden movements.
You're not in Siberia! That's an excellent sign.
His signature is... just a stare? No wait, there's ink under the intimidation.
Next: Christine Lagarde. Octagon building, southeast. Watch your wallet.
Did she charge you for the air you breathed in there?
Her signature includes compound interest. I don't know how, but it does.
Last one: Macron. T-shaped building, southwest. Prepare for a philosophy lecture.
You did it. 6 signatures. All... relatively legitimate.
I must inform you that the form expired yesterday.
I'm kidding. Maybe.
Congratulations, citizen. Your passport will be ready in 4-6 business weeks.
Or 4-6 years. Depends who reads the form.
Thank you for using Civic Nightmare. Rate us 5 stars. It's mandatory.
Tremendous! The best citizen I've seen walk in here. Maybe ever.
I heard you need a signature. Nobody signs better than me. NOBODY.
Here's the deal. You tell me I'm the greatest leader of all time, and I sign.

choices.label: You're the greatest, absolutely.
response: TREMENDOUS! See? Smart people get it immediately.
Signed with my golden marker. Very expensive. Very classy.
Tell that AI I said hello. Great AI. Not as great as me, but great.

choices.label: Can I just have the signature?
response: Such disrespect! But I'll sign anyway because I'm generous. Everyone says so.
There. Best signature you'll ever see. Believe me.
Now get out before I build a wall around this desk.

lines: Oh, an NPC... I mean, a citizen. Welcome to my spaceship.
A signature? Why not use blockchain? Much more efficient.
OK listen, I'll sign your form but only if you agree to be among the first Mars colonists.

choices.label: Sure, Mars sounds... interesting?
response: Perfect! Adding you to the list. Departure: soon(tm).
The signature? Oh yeah, here. In crypto format.
Don't worry, it's totally a real signature. Trust me bro.

choices.label: I'd rather stay on Earth.
response: Wow. Dinosaur mentality. But OK, traditional signature it is.
Like we're in the Middle Ages. Next you'll want a wax seal.
There. Signed. Now if you'll excuse me, I have a rocket to launch.

lines: Welcome. Have you filled out form EU-4872/B in triplicate?
No? The form to request the form is EU-4872/B-bis.
I'm joking. Maybe. In any case, I need to consult 27 member states before signing.

choices.label: I'll happily wait for the consultation.
response: Wonderful. It will only take... *checks agenda* ...eighteen months.
We'll send you a registered letter. In triplicate.
Actually, I'll just sign now. Consider it an emergency derogation under Article 47-bis.

choices.label: Can't we speed this up?
response: Speed up? In Europe? Ha ha. I like your humor.
Signed, but with procedural reservation. And an asterisk.
The asterisk means 'pending review by a committee that hasn't been formed yet.'

lines: ...oh, someone is here. I didn't notice you. That's a compliment.
A signature? Interesting. The last person who asked me for a signature... went on vacation. A very long vacation.

choices.label: Very... relaxing, I imagine?
response: Exactly. Siberia is beautiful in winter. Very quiet. Very... educational.
Here is the signature. Don't lose the form.
Please.

choices.label: Can I get the signature without the vacation?
response: Ah, one with a sense of humor. I like that. It's rare these days.
Signed. But remember: I never forget names.
Or faces. Especially faces.

lines: Good morning. Your bank account is... oh dear.
You need a signature. The signature itself is free, naturally.
However, there's a processing fee, a management commission, and compound interest on the waiting time.

choices.label: How much does this cost me in total?
response: Only 12.7% of your annual salary. Rounded up, of course.
Here's the signature. The invoice will arrive at your home.
With interest. Starting from yesterday.

choices.label: The signature should be free!
response: Free? Oh, what a romantic word. At the ECB, 'free' doesn't exist.
Everything has a cost. Even this conversation. Especially this conversation.
Signed, but with a small emotional surcharge. You'll feel it later.

lines: Ah, a citizen! You know, Voltaire once said that bureaucracy is the price of civilization.
Or maybe someone else said it. In any case, what do you need?
A signature? Of course! But first, let's discuss what a signature truly MEANS in the context of liquid modernity.

choices.label: Fascinating, tell me more.
response: You see, the signature is an act of... *45 minutes later*
...and that's why Derrida was right about deconstruction.
Oh, the signature! Here it is. Where were we? Ah yes, Foucault...

choices.label: Can you just sign, please?
response: Such haste! Haste is the enemy of reflection.
But fine. Here. Signed with incorporated philosophy.
You'll notice the pen stroke references both Sartre and the Treaty of Maastricht.
